---
title: "Figure 5 (macro_IR) — covariance lost by concentrating channels (fixed channel budget)"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
The LOSS side of the channel trade-off. For a fixed total channel budget B measured in blocks of N, you
run B/N independent recordings, so the pooled estimator covariance is
    cov_pooled(N)  =  var(N) / (B/N)  =  N * var(N) / B   ~  N * var(N).
If the information were linear in N (var ~ 1/N) this would be FLAT: spreading and concentrating are equal.
Since the information is sub-linear (var falls slower than 1/N), N*var(N) GROWS with N -> that growth is
exactly what you LOSE by concentrating channels (using bigger blocks) instead of spreading them.
Plotted: N*var(N) normalised to the smallest block (N=10) within each (param, interval), so y is the
multiplicative covariance penalty of the block size; y = 1 flat = no loss (linear). macro_IR, noise 0.1,
1c2ae6f, Gaussian_Distortion_Corrected_Covariance diagonal, all params, coloured by interval.
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r data}
COV      <- "Probit_statistics_Gaussian_Distortion_Corrected_Covariance"
PIDX     <- c(on = 0, off = 1, unitary_current = 2, Num_ch_mean = 5)
PARAM_LAB<- c(on = "log[10]~k[on]", off = "log[10]~k[off]",
              unitary_current = "log[10]~i", Num_ch_mean = "log[10]~N[ch]")

d <- map_dfr(Sys.glob("../data/1c2ae6f/figure_3_G_nch_*_nsim_10000_macro_IR_noise_0.1_battery_pool_G.csv"),
             ~ read.csv(.x, skip = 1, stringsAsFactors = FALSE))

dd <- d %>%
  filter(component_path == COV, statistic == "value", probit == "mean",
         param_index == param_col, param_index %in% PIDX) %>%
  mutate(param_name = names(PIDX)[match(param_index, PIDX)]) %>%
  group_by(interval_in_tau, param_name, Num_ch) %>%
  summarise(var = mean(value), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(pooled = Num_ch * var) %>%                          # covariance for a fixed channel budget (~)
  group_by(interval_in_tau, param_name) %>%
  mutate(y = pooled / pooled[Num_ch == min(Num_ch)]) %>%     # normalise to the smallest block (N=10)
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(iv_f = factor(interval_in_tau),
         param = factor(param_name, levels = names(PIDX), labels = unname(PARAM_LAB)))
```

```{r plot, fig.width = 7.5, fig.height = 5.6}
g <- ggplot(dd, aes(Num_ch, y, colour = iv_f, group = iv_f)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, colour = "grey45", linewidth = .3, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_line(linewidth = .5) + geom_point(size = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~ param, nrow = 2, labeller = label_parsed) +
  scale_x_log10(breaks = c(10, 100, 1000, 10000), labels = c("10", "100", "1e3", "1e4")) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_colour_viridis_d(name = expression(Delta / tau[min]), end = .92, direction = -1) +
  labs(x = expression(N[ch]~"per recording (block size)"),
       y = "pooled covariance penalty  N x var(N), rel. to N=10") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 9, base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), legend.position = "right",
        axis.text = element_text(size = 7.5), axis.title = element_text(size = 8.5),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 8.5))
g
ggsave("Figure_5_IR_channel_pooled_loss.pdf", g, width = 7.5, height = 5.6)
```

```{r table}
# multiplicative covariance penalty at N=10000 blocks vs N=10 blocks, median over intervals
dd %>% filter(Num_ch == 10000) %>% group_by(param_name) %>%
  summarise(penalty_10k_vs_10 = round(median(y), 1), .groups = "drop") %>% as.data.frame()
```
